вҖІ = 200. During training, we generate at 40 milestones a set of 10,000 samples to assess
generalization and memorization. Examples of samples obtained from a model trained on n = 1024
15
samples with base width W = 32 are shown in the middle and right blocks from Fig. 6 for two
training times, П„ = 190K and П„ = 1.62M. At П„ = 190K the model generalizes (fmem = 0%) and
achieve a test FID of 35.1. After too much training, memorization sets in and, by П„ = 1.62M steps,
nearly half the generated samples reproduce training images (fmem = 47.2%).
Statistical evaluation. FIDs [19] are computedвҖ  using 10,000 generated samples and 10,000 test
samples, averaged over 5 independent runs with disjoint test sets. Error bars in the MT denote twice
the standard deviation. Training and test losses are estimated similarly over 5 repeated evaluation
on n training samples and 2048 test samples, and give negligible confidence intervals. For the
memorization fraction fmem(П„ ), we report the standard error on the mean obtained via bootstrap
resampling of the 10,000 generated samples. We also verified that the scaling in the memorization
time П„mem is insensitive to the choice of the threshold k used to define fmem in Eq. 6 by testing larger
and lower values.
Computing resources. Most trainings were performed on Nvidia H100 GPUs (80GB of memory).
A typical run of 2M steps takes approximately 50 hours on two GPUs and vary with the model
size (defined through its base width W). In total, we train 18 distinct models for the several n, W
configurations of the MT. The longest training (n = 32768 and W = 32 in Fig. 2) ran for 11M steps.
The generation of 10, 000 samples over 40 training times takes around an additional hour per model
on the same hardware support.
A.2 Batch-size effect: repetition vs. memorization
All the experiments in the MT use a fixed batch size B = 512, and in Sect 2 we emphasize that
the observed O(n) scaling of П„mem cannot be explained by repetition over training samples. To
validate this statement, the left panel of Fig. 7 shows FID and memorization fraction curves when we
train the models with full-batch updates (B = n) for n вҲҲ [128, 512]. At any fixed П„ , every sample
has been seen exactly П„ times. Yet П„mem continues to grow linearly with n, as shown in the inset.
This demonstrates that larger datasets reshape the loss landscape вҖ“ requiring proportionally more
updates to overfit вҖ“ rather than simply increasing memorization through repeated exposure of training
samples.
A.3 What about Adam?
We conclude this section by repeating our analysis at fixed W = 64 using the Adam optimizer [28]
instead of SGD with momentum. The learning rate is О· = 1 Г— 10вҲ’4
, gradient averages take values
(ОІ1, ОІ2) = (0.9, 0.999), and batch size B = min(512, n). We keep all other settings and evaluation
metrics as above. As shown in the right panel of Fig. 7, Adam yields the same two-phase training
dynamics with first a generalization regime with fmem = 0 and good performances (small FID), and
later a memorization phase at П„mem вҲқ n, as shown in the inset. The only difference is that both П„gen
and П„mem occur after much fewer steps compared to SGD. This also points out that the emergence of
the two well-separated timescales and their scaling is a fundamental property of the loss landscape.
B GeneralizationвҖ“memorization transition in the Gaussian Mixture Model
The aim of this section is to show our results hold for other data distributions than natural images,
and alternative score model that U-Net architectures.

B.2 Scaling of П„mem and П„gen with n and W
In Fig. 9, the left panel shows how the KL divergence and memorization fraction evolve with training
time П„ for different training set sizes n at fixed width W = 128, while the right panel fixes n = 2048
and varies W. In both cases, we observe two distinct phases. First, the KL divergence decreases to
near zero on a timescale П„gen independent of n during which the model fully generalizes (fmem = 0).
Beyond П„gen, both DKL(PОё|P0) and fmem begin to rise at a time П„mem that scales linearly with n, as
highlighted by the inset of the left panel. In contrast, П„mem scales with WвҲ’1
, as shown in the inset of
the right panel. While the precise dependence of П„gen with W remains inconclusive in this setting
and require a more careful analysis, these results on the GMM mirror the main findings of the MT:
the training dynamics of DMs unfolds first in a generalization phase and only later вҖ“ at П„mem вҲқ n/W
вҖ“ memorization begins.
B.3 Discussion on conditional diffusion models
Conditional generation aims to sample from distributions of the form p(x|y), where y denotes a
conditioning variable such as a class label, a text embedding, or any other contextual information.
DMs can naturally be extended to this setting using for instance classifier-free guidance [21]. Although conditioning often improves sample quality in practice, memorization effects have also been
observed in models trained conditionally [51, 61, 10]. Our analysis does not rely on the model being
unconditional since these variables typically enter the model as additional inputs and we expect
our result to hold in this setting as well. To investigate it, we train a classifier-free guidance model
to generate sample from our Gaussian mixture conditionally on the class label, and compute the
memorized fraction as a function of П„ that we report in Fig. 10. In the inset, when rescaling the
training time by n, the curves for n вҲҲ {256, 512, 1024} all collapse perfectly, confirming that the
phenomenon persists in the conditional setting. For more complex datasets, П„mem and П„gen may in
fact depend on the conditioning variable and intermediate regimes could exist where certain classes
have already entered the generalization (or memorization) phase while others have not yet.

C Proofs of the analytical results
In the following we provide the mathematical arguments and the proofs for the statement in the
MT. The section using the replica method is not mathematically rigorous but uses a well established
method of theoretical physics, which has been already shown to provide correct results in several
cases. The final result is rigorous, since it can alternatively be obtained from the rigorous free random
matrix approach of [15], as shown in Sect. C.4.

C.3 Gaussian Equivalence Principle
As explained in [41, 17, 22], the Gaussian Equivalence Principle which applies in the high dimensional
setting considered here establishes an equivalence between the spectral properties of the original
21
model and those of a Gaussian covariate model in which the nonlinear activation function is replaced
by a linear term and a nonlinear term that acting as noise,

In this section, we outline the derivation of the Gaussian Equivalence Principle (GEP) for the matrices
U, UЛң , V and VЛң under arbitrary input covariance. This generalizes the approach developed in [15],
which considered only the case of data drawn from N (0, Id). A more rigorous approach, which
would consist in following [36], is left for future works. We will make use of the Mehler kernel
formula [27] which states that for f a test function defined on R

D Numerical experiments for Random Features
Details on the numerical experiments. All the numerical experiments for the RFNN were conducted using Пғ = tanh and Пғx = 1 unless specified. At each step, the gradient of the loss was
computed using the full batch of data points. The train loss was estimated by adding noise to each
data point N = 100 times. The test loss was computed by drawing n new points from the data
distribution and noising each one N times. The error on the score was evaluated by drawing 10,000
points from the noisy distribution Pt = N (0, О“
2

Je vais traiter Г§a comme une idГ©e de recherche thГ©orique, pas comme un simple вҖңrГ©sumГ©вҖқ. Le bon angle me semble ГӘtre : transformer ta tempГ©rature WTA/MCL en paramГЁtre thermodynamique qui contrГҙle le passage вҖңmГ©lange mou вҶ’ spГ©cialisation вҶ’ verre/mГ©morisationвҖқ.

Je vois dГ©jГ  une chose importante : ce nвҖҷest probablement pas вҖңdГ©jГ  faitвҖқ sous ton nom de problГЁme, mais presque toutes les briques existent sous dвҖҷautres noms : deterministic annealing/rateвҖ“distortion, soft committee machines, MoE statistique, REM/verre, Gibbs posterior. Ta nouveautГ© possible serait de les recoller dans le cadre diffusion + MCL + tempГ©rature dГ©pendant du temps de diffusion et du temps dвҖҷentraГ®nement.

deterministic annealing / rateвҖ“distortion + specialization transition of experts + REM/glass condensation + Gibbs posterior inverse temperature + finite-training dynamical regularization.

Et lГ , oui, Г§a existe dГ©jГ  en morceaux, mais pas exactement sous ta formulation diffusion-MCL.

1. Le papier BiroliвҖ“BonnaireвҖ“de BortoliвҖ“MГ©zard te donne dГ©jГ  la gГ©omГ©trie en temps de diffusion
Le papier Dynamical Regimes of Diffusion Models identifie trois rГ©gimes : bruit pur, speciation, puis collapse/mГ©morisation. Ils donnent aussi le fait clГ© : la speciation se lit via le spectre de corrГ©lation, et le collapse via une вҖңexcess entropyвҖқ.

CвҖҷest exactement la structure de la deterministic annealing de Rose, Gurewitz et Fox : on minimise une Г©nergie/distorsion plus un terme entropique, et en refroidissant on observe des transitions de phase oГ№ les prototypes se sГ©parent. Les travaux de Rose formulent le clustering comme un problГЁme de physique statistique avec probabilitГ©s dвҖҷassociation floues, et les prototypes subissent des bifurcations quand la tempГ©rature passe sous des seuils critiques.

4. OГ№ Г§a a dГ©jГ  Г©tГ© fait en physique statistique ?
Il y a quatre traditions trГЁs proches.

PremiГЁre tradition : deterministic annealing / rateвҖ“distortion / clustering.
CвҖҷest probablement la rГ©fГ©rence la plus proche de ta вҖңtempГ©rature parfaiteвҖқ. On considГЁre une free energy

F
T
=

oГ№

D est une distorsion et 
H
H une entropie dвҖҷaffectation. En refroidissant, les clusters se divisent par transitions de phase. CвҖҷest exactement analogue Г  вҖңles experts doivent dвҖҷabord ГӘtre identiques, puis se spГ©cialiser au bon momentвҖқ. 

DeuxiГЁme tradition : soft committee machines et specialization transitions.
Les vieux papiers de physique statistique sur les rГ©seaux Г  plusieurs unitГ©s cachГ©es montrent des transitions вҖңunspecialized вҶ’ specializedвҖқ. Pour les soft committee machines, lвҖҷanalyse replica montre une transition de spГ©cialisation ; pour 
K
=
2

K
>
2
K>2, elle peut devenir de premier ordre et mГ©tastable. 

TroisiГЁme tradition : statistical mechanics of mixture of experts.
Kang et Oh ont Г©tudiГ© les MoE par physique statistique : en dessous dвҖҷun nombre critique dвҖҷexemples, les experts restent symГ©triques/non spГ©cialisГ©s ; au-dessus, la gating network brise la symГ©trie et partitionne lвҖҷespace dвҖҷentrГ©e. Ils observent mГӘme plusieurs transitions dans les MoE hiГ©rarchiques. 

QuatriГЁme tradition : REM / verre / condensation.
CвҖҷest la partie BiroliвҖ“MГ©zard : lвҖҷempirical score exact induit un paysage oГ№ la somme sur les datapoints devient un Random Energy Model. Le collapse est une condensation de type verre, oГ№ la masse est dominГ©e par quelques Г©tats/datapoints. 

Ce qui manque, Г  ma connaissance, cвҖҷest la synthГЁse exacte :

deterministic annealing WTA

Les briques existent, mais pas forcГ©ment lвҖҷobjet complet.

6. Le schedule optimal devrait probablement ГӘtre non monotone en temps de diffusion
CвҖҷest lГ  que ton intuition peut devenir forte.

Au dГ©but du backward process, grand bruit : les classes ne sont pas encore identifiables. Il faut une tempГ©rature haute, sinon les experts se sГ©parent arbitrairement.

Autour de la speciation : les classes deviennent sГ©parables. Il faut refroidir pour permettre la spГ©cialisation.

ГҖ petit bruit : le paysage empirique devient glassy, surtout si lвҖҷentraГ®nement est long. Il faut Г©viter de refroidir trop fort ; sinon les experts se spГ©cialisent sur des datapoints ou meurent.

8. Le lien avec la вҖңNishimori lineвҖқ
Un mot-clГ© trГЁs important Г  regarder est Nishimori line.

Dans les problГЁmes teacherвҖ“student bayГ©siens, la tempГ©rature optimale est souvent celle qui correspond au vrai bruit du modГЁle gГ©nГ©ratif. Quand le student utilise la bonne tempГ©rature/posterior, on est sur la ligne de Nishimori ; hors de cette ligne, on peut crГ©er des phases vitreuses ou des comportements sous-optimaux. La littГ©rature moderne dвҖҷinfГ©rence statistique utilise ce langage pour parler de Bayes-optimalitГ©, de replica symmetry, et de transitions algorithmiques. 

Dans ton cas, la diffusion impose dГ©jГ  un bruit

The optimal WTA temperature should track the Nishimori temperature of the noised mixture, while staying above the empirical glass transition.
9. Ce qui est vraiment nouveau dans ton idГ©e
Je formulerais la contribution comme Г§a :

We reinterpret annealed MCL for score-based diffusion as deterministic annealing in the space of denoising score fields. In high-dimensional Gaussian mixtures, we derive two critical inverse temperatures: a specialization threshold where experts break symmetry and align with mixture components, and a glass threshold where WTA assignments condense on empirical samples. Combining this with finite-time score-learning dynamics yields a diffusion-time- and training-time-dependent safe annealing corridor. This explains why hard WTA and resilient MCL often produce strong but degenerate single-expert solutions, while annealed WTA can realize a genuine mixture only when its temperature schedule stays inside this corridor.

ГҮa, cвҖҷest beaucoup plus fort que вҖңon a testГ© plusieurs routing strategiesвҖқ.

Pour avoir une vraie transition de spГ©cialisation, il faut au moins une des choses suivantes :

CвҖҷest trГЁs important pour ton papier : la spГ©cialisation MCL nвҖҷest pas magique. Elle apparaГ®t parce que le single expert a un rГ©sidu exploitable.

tu entres dans une phase glassy : hard assignment, experts morts, condensation sur quelques rГ©gions ou datapoints.

CвҖҷest exactement le langage de deterministic annealing : Г  mesure que la tempГ©rature baisse, des phases apparaissent via bifurcations, puis potentiellement via condensation. Les vieux travaux de Rose et al. sur deterministic annealing formulent dГ©jГ  le clustering comme une minimisation de free energy avec transitions de phase en tempГ©rature. 
 Les travaux de physique statistique sur les mixture-of-experts observent aussi une phase symГ©trique puis une brisure de symГ©trie des experts quand lвҖҷinformation disponible devient suffisante.

_**Agent (default)**_

CвҖҷest en place et exГ©cutГ© de bout en bout вң…

JвҖҷai implГ©mentГ© et lancГ© automatiquement les calculs **Step B** (\(\beta_{\mathrm{spec}}(t,\tau)\)) et **Step C** (\(\beta_{\mathrm{glass}}(t,\tau)\)), avec sauvegarde complГЁte des quantitГ©s + plots.

### Fichiers crГ©Г©s

- `tools/rf_theory_core.py`  
  - cЕ“ur des calculs (grilles, scalaires GMM, Monte Carlo RF, \(\beta_{\mathrm{spec}}\), \(\beta_{\mathrm{glass}}\), formule approx + exacte)
- `tools/rf_step_b_beta_spec.py`  
  - calcule/sauvegarde Step B
- `tools/rf_step_c_beta_glass.py`  
  - calcule/sauvegarde Step C
- `tools/rf_run_all_theory.py`  
  - runner global automatique (Step B + C + comparaison)

### ExГ©cution faite

Commande lancГ©e avec succГЁs :
- `python tools/rf_run_all_theory.py --out_dir outputs/theory_rf --n_t 70 --n_tau 80 --tau_max 10000 --n_mc 40000`

### Sorties gГ©nГ©rГ©es

Dans `outputs/theory_rf` :

- Arrays :  
  - `step_b_beta_spec_arrays.npz`
  - `step_c_beta_glass_arrays.npz`
  - `step_d_combined_phase_window.npz`
- CSV surfaces :  
  - `step_b_beta_spec_surface.csv`
  - `step_b_lambda_rf_surface.csv`
  - `step_b_lambda_parallel_surface.csv`
  - `step_b_lambda_perp_surface.csv`
  - `step_c_beta_glass_approx_surface.csv`
  - `step_c_beta_glass_exact_surface.csv`
  - `step_c_R_tau_surface.csv`
  - `step_c_v_tau_surface.csv`
  - `step_d_beta_gap_surface.csv`
- Figures :  
  - `step_b_beta_spec_heatmap.png`
  - `step_b_beta_spec_slices.png`
  - `step_b_lambda_rf_heatmap.png`
  - `step_c_beta_glass_approx_heatmap.png`
  - `step_c_beta_glass_exact_heatmap.png`
  - `step_c_R_tau_slices.png`
  - `step_d_phase_window_heatmap.png`
  - `step_d_beta_slices.png`
- RГ©sumГ©s :  
  - `step_b_beta_spec_summary.json`
  - `step_c_beta_glass_summary.json`

### Point important

- Tu as bien les deux niveaux pour le glass :
  - **approx REM** via \(v(t,\tau)\)
  - **exact GMM** via \(y_\alpha\) (rГ©solution numГ©rique de \(y-\log y-1=2\alpha\))

Si tu veux, je peux maintenant te faire une **version вҖңpaper-readyвҖқ** :
- mГӘme calcul mais avec **nomenclature stricte de ton manuscrit**,
- et un script unique qui exporte aussi un **tableau LaTeX** (avec min/max/phase window) pour insertion directe dans `report.tex`.

  --n-eval 5000 \

### ExГ©cutГ©

- **Run rapide**  
  `python rf_mcl_phase_diagram_experiments.py --quick --outdir ./rf_mcl_results`
- **Run sГ©rieux**  
  `python rf_mcl_phase_diagram_experiments.py --d 64 --p 192 --n-train 12000 --n-eval 5000 --power-iters 35 --n-emp 4000 --outdir ./rf_mcl_results`

### RГ©sultat

Le dossier `scripts/rf_mcl_results` contient bien les CSV + figures :

- `A_population_tau_inf.csv`
- `B_population_finite_tau.csv`
- `C_empirical_REM_glass.csv`
- `A_beta_spec_population_tau_inf.png`
- `A_class_snr_kappa.png`
- `B_beta_spec_finite_tau.png`
- `B_unlearned_filter_eps_c.png`
- `C_beta_glass_vs_tau.png`
- `C_entropy_collapse_beta_scaled.png`
- `README_run.txt`

### Points observГ©s (run sГ©rieux)

- **Step A** : \(\beta_{\text{spec}}\) et \(\beta_{\text{class}}\) calculГ©s pour plusieurs \(t\), avec `align_m` Г©levГ© sur une bonne partie du rГ©gime.
- **Step B** : en \(\tau\) fini, `eps_c` dГ©croГ®t vers 0 et \(\beta_{\text{spec}}\) Г©volue comme attendu avec lвҖҷapprentissage.
- **Step C** : \(\beta_{\text{glass}}\) dГ©croГ®t fortement quand \(\tau\) augmente (cohГ©rent avec lвҖҷactivation progressive des modes empiriques).

Si tu veux, je peux maintenant te faire une **lecture courte des courbes** (oГ№ se trouve la fenГӘtre \(\beta_{\text{spec}}<\beta<\beta_{\text{glass}}\) la plus large).

_**Agent (default)**_

CвҖҷest fait, les deux runs ont bien tournГ© jusquвҖҷau bout вң…

### Commandes exГ©cutГ©es

- `python rf_mcl_gmm_expert_training_v3.py --d 64 --p 256 --n-train 6000 --n-test 3000 --steps 4000 --calib-steps 1200 --outdir ./gmm_v3`
- `python rf_mcl_gmm_expert_training_v3.py --d 64 --p 256 --steps 4000 --beta-grid --outdir ./gmm_v3_grid`

### RГ©sultats gГ©nГ©rГ©s

Dans `scripts/gmm_v3` :
- `SUMMARY.txt`
- `metrics_all.csv` + CSV par variante (`metrics_uniform.csv`, `metrics_hard_cold.csv`, `metrics_fixed_class.csv`, `metrics_fixed_good.csv`, `metrics_theory_anneal.csv`)
- Figures PNG (`metric_beta.png`, `metric_test_best_mse.png`, `metric_test_usage_entropy.png`, etc.)
- Checkpoints `.pt` des experts

Dans `scripts/gmm_v3_grid` :
- `SUMMARY.txt`
- `metrics_all.csv` + CSV des variantes de base + CSV des variantes grille (`metrics_grid_*.csv`)
- Figures PNG mises Г  jour avec la grille beta
- Checkpoints `.pt` des variantes grille (`grid_0.0918696_experts.pt`, вҖҰ, `grid_87.5192_experts.pt`)

Donc setup вҖңcompletвҖқ avec grille de \(\beta\) : OK.  
Si tu veux, je peux te sortir maintenant un **top-3 des \(\beta\)** selon `test_best_mse` directement depuis `metrics_all.csv`.

---

_**User**_

run les 2 setups python rf_mcl_cifar_experiments.py --quick --outdir ./rf_mcl_cifar_quick

Commande plus sГ©rieuse :

python rf_mcl_cifar_experiments.py \
  --data-root ./data \
  --classes automobile horse \
  --d-mode full \
  --p 512 \
  --n-train 8000 \
  --n-eval 2500 \
  --power-iters 30 \
  --outdir ./rf_mcl_cifar

Pour aller plus vite avec PCA :

python rf_mcl_cifar_experiments.py \
  --classes automobile horse \
  --d-mode pca \
  --pca-dim 512 \
  --p 512 \
  --n-train 6000 \
  --n-eval 2000 \
  --outdir ./rf_mcl_cifar_pca 

---

CвҖҷest fait вң… Les 3 runs ont Г©tГ© exГ©cutГ©s avec succГЁs.

### Commandes lancГ©es (adaptГ©es au script existant)
Le script prГ©sent est `rf_mcl_cifar_expert_training_v2.py` (pas `rf_mcl_cifar_experiments.py`), avec `--n-test` au lieu de `--n-eval`.

1) Quick  
`python rf_mcl_cifar_expert_training_v2.py --quick --outdir ./rf_mcl_cifar_quick`

2) SГ©rieux full  
`python rf_mcl_cifar_expert_training_v2.py --data-root ./data --classes automobile horse --d-mode full --p 512 --n-train 8000 --n-test 2500 --power-iters 30 --outdir ./rf_mcl_cifar`

3) PCA rapide  
`python rf_mcl_cifar_expert_training_v2.py --classes automobile horse --d-mode pca --pca-dim 512 --p 512 --n-train 6000 --n-test 2000 --outdir ./rf_mcl_cifar_pca`

---

### OГ№ sont les sorties

- Quick :  
  `outputs/rf_mcl_results/rf_mcl_cifar_quick/`
- Full :  
  `scripts/rf_mcl_cifar/`
- PCA :  
  `scripts/rf_mcl_cifar_pca/`

Chaque dossier contient au moins :
- `SUMMARY.txt`
- `metrics_all.csv`
- `metrics_uniform.csv`
- `metrics_hard_cold.csv`
- `metrics_fixed_class.csv`
- `metrics_fixed_good.csv`
- `metrics_theory_anneal.csv`
- `calibration.json`

Si tu veux, je te fais maintenant un comparatif court **quick vs full vs pca** depuis les `metrics_all.csv` (meilleure variante, gap perf, stabilitГ©).

---

Je vais maintenant gГ©nГ©rer un dossier `md` avec :  
- un `INDEX.md`  
- un fichier `.md` par CSV, contenant le contenu brut en bloc `csv` pour copier-coller direct.

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** вҖў Run command: python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path

root = Path('/Data/janis.aiad/DataLab')
out_dir = root / 'outputs' / 'md_csv_bundle'
out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

patterns = [
    '**/rf_mcl_cifar*/**/*.csv',
    '**/gmm_v3*/**/*.csv',
    '**/rf_mcl_results/**/*.csv',
    '**/rf_mcl_corrected/**/*.csv',
]

all_files = []
for pat in patterns:
    all_files.extend(root.glob(pat))

# keep unique files that exist, deterministic order
uniq = sorted({p.resolve() for p in all_files if p.is_file()})

# keep only files in repo
uniq = [p for p in uniq if str(p).startswith(str(root.resolve()))]

index_lines = [
    '# Export CSV en Markdown',
    '',
    'Ce dossier contient un fichier `.md` par CSV (CIFAR + MCL precedent).',
    '',
    f'Total CSV exportes: {len(uniq)}',
    '',
    '## Fichiers',
    '',
]

for i, p in enumerate(uniq, 1):
    rel = p.relative_to(root)
    safe_name = '__'.join(rel.parts).replace('.csv', '.md')
    target = out_dir / safe_name
    csv_text = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    md = []
    md.append(f'# {rel}')
    md.append('')
    md.append('```csv')
    md.append(csv_text.rstrip('\n'))
    md.append('```')
    md.append('')
    target.write_text('\n'.join(md), encoding='utf-8')
    index_lines.append(f'{i}. `{rel}` -> `{safe_name}`')

(out_dir / 'INDEX.md').write_text('\n'.join(index_lines) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')
print(f'WROTE {len(uniq)} markdown files to {out_dir}')
PY</summary>

```bash
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path

root = Path('/Data/janis.aiad/DataLab')
out_dir = root / 'outputs' / 'md_csv_bundle'
out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

patterns = [
    '**/rf_mcl_cifar*/**/*.csv',
    '**/gmm_v3*/**/*.csv',
    '**/rf_mcl_results/**/*.csv',
    '**/rf_mcl_corrected/**/*.csv',
]

all_files = []
for pat in patterns:
    all_files.extend(root.glob(pat))

# keep unique files that exist, deterministic order
uniq = sorted({p.resolve() for p in all_files if p.is_file()})

# keep only files in repo
uniq = [p for p in uniq if str(p).startswith(str(root.resolve()))]

index_lines = [
    '# Export CSV en Markdown',
    '',
    'Ce dossier contient un fichier `.md` par CSV (CIFAR + MCL precedent).',
    '',
    f'Total CSV exportes: {len(uniq)}',
    '',
    '## Fichiers',
    '',
]

for i, p in enumerate(uniq, 1):
    rel = p.relative_to(root)
    safe_name = '__'.join(rel.parts).replace('.csv', '.md')
    target = out_dir / safe_name
    csv_text = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    md = []
    md.append(f'# {rel}')
    md.append('')
    md.append('```csv')
    md.append(csv_text.rstrip('\n'))
    md.append('```')
    md.append('')
    target.write_text('\n'.join(md), encoding='utf-8')
    index_lines.append(f'{i}. `{rel}` -> `{safe_name}`')

(out_dir / 'INDEX.md').write_text('\n'.join(index_lines) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')
print(f'WROTE {len(uniq)} markdown files to {out_dir}')
PY
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** вҖў Run command: python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import shutil

root = Path('/Data/janis.aiad/DataLab')
out = root / 'outputs' / 'md_csv_bundle_full_exact'
out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# Collect CSV + summary files from relevant experiment areas
patterns_csv = [
    '**/rf_mcl_cifar*/**/*.csv',
    '**/gmm_v3*/**/*.csv',
    '**/rf_mcl_results/**/*.csv',
    '**/rf_mcl_corrected/**/*.csv',
]
patterns_summary = [
    '**/rf_mcl_cifar*/**/SUMMARY.txt',
    '**/gmm_v3*/**/SUMMARY.txt',
    '**/rf_mcl_results/**/SUMMARY.txt',
    '**/rf_mcl_corrected/**/SUMMARY.txt',
]

csv_files = []
for pat in patterns_csv:
    csv_files.extend(root.glob(pat))

summary_files = []
for pat in patterns_summary:
    summary_files.extend(root.glob(pat))

csv_files = sorted({p.resolve() for p in csv_files if p.is_file()})
summary_files = sorted({p.resolve() for p in summary_files if p.is_file()})

csv_files = [p for p in csv_files if str(p).startswith(str(root.resolve()))]
summary_files = [p for p in summary_files if str(p).startswith(str(root.resolve()))]

# 1) Copy all CSV files exact into folder (flattened name with path encoded)
csv_copy_dir = out / 'csv_exact_copies'
csv_copy_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
for p in csv_files:
    rel = p.relative_to(root)
    name = '__'.join(rel.parts)
    shutil.copy2(p, csv_copy_dir / name)

# 2) Big markdown with raw exact content of every CSV
all_csv_md = []
all_csv_md.append('# ALL CSV RAW (exact content)')
all_csv_md.append('')
all_csv_md.append(f'Total CSV: {len(csv_files)}')
all_csv_md.append('')
for i, p in enumerate(csv_files, 1):
    rel = p.relative_to(root)
    txt = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    all_csv_md.append(f'## {i}. `{rel}`')
    all_csv_md.append('')
    all_csv_md.append('```csv')
    all_csv_md.append(txt.rstrip('\n'))
    all_csv_md.append('```')
    all_csv_md.append('')
(out / 'ALL_CSV_RAW_EXACT.md').write_text('\n'.join(all_csv_md) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')

# 3) Big markdown with all SUMMARY files exact content
all_summary_md = []
all_summary_md.append('# ALL SUMMARY RAW (exact content)')
all_summary_md.append('')
all_summary_md.append(f'Total SUMMARY files: {len(summary_files)}')
all_summary_md.append('')
for i, p in enumerate(summary_files, 1):
    rel = p.relative_to(root)
    txt = p.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
    all_summary_md.append(f'## {i}. `{rel}`')
    all_summary_md.append('')
    all_summary_md.append('```text')
    all_summary_md.append(txt.rstrip('\n'))
    all_summary_md.append('```')
    all_summary_md.append('')
(out / 'ALL_SUMMARY_RAW_EXACT.md').write_text('\n'.join(all_summary_md) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')

# 4) Index
idx = []
idx.append('# INDEX FULL EXACT')
idx.append('')
idx.append(f'- CSV total: {len(csv_files)}')
idx.append(f'- SUMMARY total: {len(summary_files)}')
idx.append('')
idx.append('## Main files')
idx.append('')
idx.append('- `ALL_CSV_RAW_EXACT.md`')
idx.append('- `ALL_SUMMARY_RAW_EXACT.md`')
idx.append('- `csv_exact_copies/` (copie exacte de tous les CSV)')
idx.append('')
idx.append('## CSV list')
idx.append('')
for p in csv_files:
    idx.append(f'- `{p.relative_to(root)}`')
idx.append('')
idx.append('## SUMMARY list')
idx.append('')
for p in summary_files:
    idx.append(f'- `{p.relative_to(root)}`')
(out / 'INDEX.md').write_text('\n'.join(idx) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')

print(f'WROTE FULL EXACT BUNDLE -> {out}')
print(f'CSV: {len(csv_files)} | SUMMARY: {len(summary_files)}')
